content: I read Vinterhamn over the weekend after seeing this post and I completely understand what you mean by trusting silence. Extraordinary book.
Anders Lilja is so underrated outside Sweden - this recommendation made me finally pick it up and I finished it in two sittings.
Agreed with Birgitta - I gave it to my father and he called me to talk about it for an hour.
I have been meaning to read Nora Clement and this evening sounds like the perfect occasion - will you be recording it at all for those of us who cannot make it?
Reserved my seat - so looking forward to this, poetry evenings at Weaver & Book are always the best.
This interview is wonderful - Sofia Nyman's comment about starting with a sensation rather than a story is exactly what her novels feel like to read.
The comparison to Stridsberg is spot on and I am glad someone has finally written about it properly.
Professor Lindén's point about the body as archive is something I keep thinking about - have ordered Sofia's novel.
Congratulations on twelve years - the shop has become such an important part of this neighbourhood.
Happy anniversary! My reading life has genuinely changed since Weaver & Book opened and I mean that.
Already in the diary - the last Anders Lilja evening was one of the best things I have been to this year.
Will Astrid Lindén's introduction be posted online afterwards? It would be wonderful to have a written version.
The Kowalska recommendation is exactly right - I gave it to three people last Christmas and everyone loved it.
This gift guide is so useful - coming in on Saturday to pick up two of these.
I had no idea Granris had been going since 2008 - the quality of their list is extraordinary and this piece explains a lot.
The paragraph about stocking only what you believe in is why I drive across town to shop with you instead of ordering online.
Beautifully written - please do keep writing these longer pieces, they matter.
Such a relief to read this - I had heard some rumours and was genuinely worried. Uppsala needs this shop.
So relieved to hear this. Thank you for being honest about the uncertainty - it makes the good news even better.
I have been waiting for someone to do a proper event around Stridsberg's poetry and this is exactly what I hoped for.
Will this be ticketed or is it first come first seated? I want to make sure I get there early enough.
The change to credit translators on shelf notes is so simple and so right - I hope other shops follow your lead.
The lighthouse book description has sold me immediately - coming in tomorrow to pick it up.
